Dear Islamic brothers! Sayyidunā Sa’d  /َaininitial/uni2006/superscriptalef/lamwithalefmaksuraisolated/aleffinal/َainmedial/َtehinitial/uni2006/ُallahisolated/arabicalef/uni2006/َِg669/َreh /tatweel  /ُhehfinal/sukun/noonmedial declared that 
the well was dug for Sa’d’s mother, which meant that the 
digging of well was aimed at donating Šawāb to the mother of 
Sa’d  /َaininitial/uni2006/superscriptalef/lamwithalefmaksuraisolated/aleffinal/َainmedial/َtehinitial/uni2006/ُallahisolated/arabicalef/uni2006/َِg669/َreh /tatweel  /ُhehfinal/sukun/noonmedial. 
The foregoing narration also made it clear that there is no 
harm for the Muslims in relating a goat or cow etc. to saints 
by saying sentences like, ‘ This is the goat of Sayyidunā Ghauš 
Pāk  /َaininitial/uni2006/superscriptalef/lamwithalefmaksuraisolated/aleffinal/َainmedial/َtehinitial/uni2006/ُallahisolated/arabicalef/uni2006/َِg669/َreh /tatweel  /ُhehfinal/sukun/noonmedial’ as it also means that the sacrifice of this goat is 
aimed at just donating its Šawāb to Sayyidunā Ghauš-ul-A’ /uni1E93am 
 /َaininitial/uni2006/superscriptalef/lamwithalefmaksuraisolated/aleffinal/َainmedial/َtehinitial/uni2006/ُallahisolated/arabicalef/uni2006/َِg669/َreh /tatweel  /ُhehfinal/sukun/noonmedial. 
Normally, people also relate sacrificial animals to each other; 
for example, if you ask a person who is wandering along with 
his sacrificial cow as to whose the cow is; he will reply, ‘Mine’ 
and it is not considered objectionable either. Therefore, if there 
is no objection to saying such sentences, no objection can be 
raised to saying ‘ This is the goat of Ghauš Pāk ’ as well.  In fact, 
Allah  /َshadda/lamfinal/َjeeminitial/َوَshadda/zainfinal/َaininitial is the real Owner of each and every thing. 
Moreover, whether it is the sacrificial cow or the goat of 
Ghauš Pāk, the name of Allah  /َshadda/lamfinal/َjeeminitial/َوَshadda/zainfinal/َaininitial is mentioned at the time of 
slaughter of each sacrificial animal. May Allah  /َshadda/lamfinal/َjeeminitial/َوَshadda/zainfinal/َaininitial remove 
satanic whispers!

387 18 Madanī Pearls of Donating Šawāb  
1. One may make the Ī /uni1E63āl Šawāb of each and every deed such 
a
s Far/uni1E0D, Wājib, Sunnaĥ, Nafl, /uni1E62alāĥ, fasting, Zakāĥ, /uni1E24ajj, 
d
elivering a speech or Dars, travelling with a Madanī Qāfilaĥ, 
acting upon Madanī In‘āmāt, call towards righteousness, 
studying a religious book or making individual effort etc. 
2. Holding gatherings for the recitation of the Holy Qu rān 
and serving food to participants on the 3rd, 10th and 40th 
day of someone’s death or solemnizing his death-
anniversary are all commendable deeds, as these are also 
means of Ī/uni1E63āl Šawāb. 
B
y Sharī’aĥ, the absence of the proof of impermissibility 
about holding such rites is itself a proof of permissibility. 
Moreover, living people making supplication for the 
deceased is proven by the Holy Qurān and the whole 
concept of Ī /uni1E63āl Šawāb is based on this Qurānic proof. 
T
herefore, verse 10 of Sūraĥ al- /uni1E24ashr, part 28 says:

3. The expenses for the meals served on Sawyam (the 3rd day 
of demise), death anniversary etc. can be paid from the 
bequest of the deceased provided all the inheritors are

388 adult and they all give consent as well. If even a single

a
dult inheritor can arrange for the meals from his own 
share. (Derived from Baĥār-e-Sharī’atz vol. 1z part-IVz pp. 822)  
4. If the family of the deceased cook food on Sawyam, o nly 
the Faqīrs (destitute people) may eat from it (the rich 
should not). (ibidz P853)  
5. Ī/uni1E63āl Šawāb may be made even to a day’s old deceased ba by; 
Sawyam etc. may also be held. 
6. Šawāb may also be offered to the living Muslims and even 
to those who have not yet been born. 
7. Šawāb may be donated to Muslim Jinns as well. 
8
. Solemnizing Giyārĥwīn, Rajabī (the death anniversary  of 
Sayyidunā Imām Ja’far /uni1E62ādiq  /َaininitial/uni2006/superscriptalef/lamwithalefmaksuraisolated/aleffinal/َainmedial/َtehinitial/uni2006/ُallahisolated/arabicalef/uni2006/َِg669/َreh /tatweel  /ُhehfinal/sukun/noonmedial on the 22nd of

pudding in a ‘Kūn /uni1E0Bā’ (an earthenware pot used to serve) 
o
nly; it may be served in other plates etc. as well. It may 
also be brought out of the house.

‘Na/uni1E93r-o-Niyāz’ (in Urdu). This Niyāz (meal) is ‘Tabarruk ’ 
(sacred) and may be consumed by the rich as well as the 
poor. 
10. Serving the meal of Ī /uni1E63āl Šawāb to guests is not a condition; 
i
f the family members eat it themselves, there is no harm 
in doing so.

the meal he eats each time, it would be wonderful. For

16. Even if Šawāb is donated to innumerable Muslims, it is 
hoped by the grace of Allah  /َshadda/lamfinal/َjeeminitial/َوَshadda/zainfinal/َaininitial that complete Šawāb 
will be given to each of them. It is not that Šawāb will be 
divided amongst them. (Rad-dul-Mu/uni1E25tārz vol. 3z pp. 180z derived 
from  Baĥār-e-Sharī’atz vol. 1z part-IVz pp. 850)  
17. The Šawāb of the one donating Šawāb is not reduced a t 
all; rather, it is hoped that he would get Šawāb equal to 
the total amount of Šawāb he donated to each and every 
person. For example, someone performed an act of piety 
for which he was given (the Šawāb of) ten good deeds 
which he donated to ten other deceased Muslims. Now, 
each of the deceased would get ten good deeds and the 
sender of Šawāb would get one hundred and ten. If he 
donated Šawāb to one thousand deceased, he would get 
ten thousand and ten and so forth. (Baĥār-e-Sharī’atz pp. 850z 
vol. 1z part-IV)

awāb to a disbeliever or an apostate (Murtad) or calling 
them ‘Mar/uni1E25ūm’ is Kufr (blasphemy).

The intention of the heart is sufficient for the donation of 
Šawāb. For instance, if you give a amount of money as charity 
or recite /uni1E62alāt-‘Alan-Nabī once or tell someone a Sunnaĥ or car ry

that I have just told reach the Holy Prophet  /meemfinal/َshadda/lammedial/َseeninitial/وَuni2006/uni0656/hehfinal/ِlaminitial/superscriptalef/arabicalef/وَuni2006/ِhehfinal/sukun/yehmedial/َlammedial/َg651/uni2006/superscriptalef/lamwithalefmaksuraisolated/aleffinal/َainmedial/َtehinitial/uni2006/ُallahisolated/arabicalef/uni2006/َshadda /g644/َsadinitial!’

companions  /َaininitial /tatweel  /sukun/نarabicalef/َwawfinal/sukun/dadinitial/shadda/ِrehfinal/laminitial/arabicalef/uni2006/ُmeemfinal/ِhehmedial/sukun/yehmedial/َlammedial as mentioned in the /uni1E24adīš which

Method of Supplication for Donating Šawāb 
Yā Allah  /َshadda/lamfinal/َjeeminitial/َوَshadda/zainfinal/َaininitial! Bless us with the Šawāb of whatever has been 
recited (if food etc. is present, then also say) and the Šawāb of 
the food that has been prepared and whatever other deeds we 
have performed to date, not according to our imperfect deeds, 
but according to Your unlimited Mercy; and send its Šawāb 
on behalf of us to the court of Your Beloved /meemfinal/َshadda/lammedial/َseeninitial/وَuni2006/uni0656/hehfinal/ِlaminitial/superscriptalef/arabicalef/وَuni2006/ِhehfinal/sukun/yehmedial/َlammedial/َg651/uni2006/superscriptalef/lamwithalefmaksuraisolated/aleffinal/َainmedial/َtehinitial/uni2006/ُallahisolated/arabicalef/uni2006/َshadda /g644/َsadinitial. 
Through Your Beloved Prophet /meemfinal/َshadda/lammedial/َseeninitial/وَuni2006/uni0656/hehfinal/ِlaminitial/superscriptalef/arabicalef/وَuni2006/ِhehfinal/sukun/yehmedial/َlammedial/َg651/uni2006/superscriptalef/lamwithalefmaksuraisolated/aleffinal/َainmedial/َtehinitial/uni2006/ُallahisolated/arabicalef/uni2006 /َshadda /g644/َsadinitial, send its 
Šawāb to all other Prophets  /ِhehmedial/sukun/yehmedial/َlammedial/َg651 /َم lamwithaleffinal/َshadda/seenmedial/laminitial/arabicalef/uni2006/ُmeemfinal, all the blessed companions 
 /sukun/نarabicalef/َwawfinal/sukun/dadinitial/shadda/ِrehfinal/laminitial/arabicalef/uni2006/ُmeemfinal/ِhehmedial/sukun/yehmedial/َlammedial/َg651, and all the noble saints  /superscriptalef/lamwithalefmaksuraisolated/aleffinal/َainmedial/َtehinitial/uni2006/ُg569/g568/arabicalef/uni2006/ُmeemfinal/ُhehmedial/َِhahwithmeeminitial/َreh. Through Your 
Beloved Prophet /meemfinal/َshadda/lammedial/َseeninitial/وَuni2006/uni0656/hehfinal/ِlaminitial/superscriptalef/arabicalef/وَuni2006/ِhehfinal/sukun/yehmedial/َlammedial/َg651/uni2006/superscriptalef/lamwithalefmaksuraisolated/aleffinal/َainmedial/َtehinitial/uni2006/ُallahisolated/arabicalef/uni2006/َshadda /g644/َsadinitial send its Šawāb to every Muslim 
human and Jinn born from the age of Sayyidunā Adam /َمlamwithaleffinal/َshadda/seenmedial/laminitial/arabicalef/uni2006/ِhehfinal/sukun/yehmedial/َlammedial/َg651 
to date as well as all those who will be born till the Day of 
Judgement. 
During this, mention the names of the saints to whom Šawāb 
has to be especially donated. Likewise, donate Šawāb to your 
parents, relatives and Murshid (spiritual guide). (The deceased 
whose names are mentioned during the supplication get 
pleased by it). Then, finish the supplication as usual. (If a 
small amount of each type of food was placed in front with the 
water, then mix them back into the other food and water).

397 Beware! 
Whenever Niyāz or any other type of ceremony is held at your 
home and the time of congregational /uni1E62alāĥ approaches during 
t
he ceremony, take all the guests to the Masjid for /uni1E62alāĥ with 
J
amā’at making individual effort provided there is no Shar’ī 
prohibition. 
Ceremonies should be scheduled at such a time that the time 
of /uni1E62alāĥ does not fall within the scheduled program so t hat 
the participants would not miss Jamā’at due to laziness. There 
will probably be no difficulty in offering /uni1E62alāĥ with Jamā’at if 
l
unch is served immediately after /uni1E92uĥar /uni1E62alāĥ or dinner is served 
a
fter ‘Ishā-/uni1E62alāĥ. Even then, if the time of /uni1E62alāĥ approaches, 
t
he host, the cook, the guest and those involved in serving the 
food should all offer /uni1E62alāĥ with Jamā’at. Missing the Jamā’at 
o
f /uni1E62alāĥ just for Niyāz of saints is a grave blunder.

Method of Visiting the Shrines  
One should visit the court of the saints from the direction of 
their feet, because if coming from the opposite side, they 
would have to turn their head to look back. Hence, one should 
present himself at the shrines of the saints from the direction 
of the feet of the saint facing the head and making his back 
face the Qiblaĥ. Thereafter, he should stand at a distance of at 
least two yards and say Salām in the following words.

 When Should One Perform Cautionary 
Renewal of Faith? 
It is a Madanī suggestion to perform cautionary repentance 
and renewal of faith at least once everyday (for example 
before sleeping). If witnesses are readily available, a husband 
and wife should also perform repentance and a cautionary 
renewal of Nikah [marriage] from time to time. Mother, 
father, brother, sister, children etc. – any sane, adult male 
or female can be a witness for Nikah. Cautionary renewal 
of Nikah is completely free – there is no requirement of 
Maĥr [dowry] for this.

 Four from Forty Thousand 
Sayyidunā ‘Abdullaĥ Ibn Mubārak /hehfinal/sukun/yehmedial/َlammedial/َg651/uni2006/superscriptalef/lamwithalefmaksuraisolated/aleffinal/َainmedial/َtehinitial/uni2006/ِallahisolated/arabicalef/uni2006/ُtehmarbutafinal/َsukun/hahwithmeeminitial/َreh narrated 
that a wise man chose the following four pieces of advice 
from forty thousand. 
1.  Do not trust every woman in every matter. 
2.  Never rely on your wealth. 
3.  Do not over-burden your stomach (by overeating). 
4.  Do not acquire such knowledge that does not benefit 
you, like (information, news etc.). 
 (Al-Munabbiĥāt lil ‘Asqalānīz p. 47)
